data.title: Global X taps into chips and quantum trends for its latest thematic ETF filing
data.description:
Global X, a leading thematic exchange traded fund provider with roughly $100B in assets under management, is preparing to launch a new fund targeting two cutting-edge areas of technology: semiconductors and quantum computing.

A recent filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission shows that the firm plans to introduce the Global X AI Semiconductor & Quantum ETF, which is expected to trade under the ticker symbol CHPX. The fund will be passively managed and come with a total annual expense ratio of 0.50%. 

CHPX is designed to track companies operating across the artificial intelligence semiconductor and quantum computing ecosystems. The ETF’s underlying index will be built around four sub-themes: 

AI Semiconductors: Firms developing GPUs, CPUs, ASICs, memory, networking chips, and other components essential for AI training and inference. 

Compute Systems Enablers: Companies providing AI-focused hardware and software systems, including servers, networking equipment, and next-generation data center solutions.

Data Center Infrastructure: Businesses delivering cooling systems, HVAC, and power management technologies to improve efficiency in AI data centers. 

Quantum Computing Technologies: Companies working on quantum systems that aim to solve problems beyond the reach of traditional computing. 

The launch underscores Global X’s ongoing push to expand its suite of thematic ETFs and tap investor demand for exposure to fast-growing technologies shaping the future of computing.

While no direct holdings were listed, its quite possible that CHPX will be filled with leading chip and quantum focused stocks suchas Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Intel (INTC), Quantum Computing (QUBT), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), IIonQ (IONQ), and others.

data.title: U.S. stocks uplifted by Intel-Nvidia deal, a day after Fed's 25 bp rate cut
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Thursday, with the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) and the S&P 500 (SP500) rallying, a day after the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.6% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.3%.

Markets were helped by strong premarket action from tech firms Intel (INTC) +24.6% and Nvidia (NVDA) +2.5% after the firms announced a blockbuster deal on Thursday to co-develop PC and data center chips. Simultaneously, Nvidia said it would take a $5B stake in Intel.

Also, the FOMC on Wednesday announced its decision, trimming its policy rate by 25 basis points to 4.00%-4.25% on Wednesday. Miran dissented, arguing for a 50-basis-point cut.

“The Federal Reserve is cutting interest rates during a time when stocks are at record highs and the economy is still growing, which is a very unique backdrop, as Fed cuts are typically associated with a problematic economy. This dynamic is bullish for stocks,” said Robert Schein, CIO at Blanke Schein Wealth Management.

“Now that rates have been moving lower and the Federal Reserve is looking to cut rates a few more times, we are even more bullish on big tech and financials stocks. Big tech stocks tend to outperform during lower interest rate environments, and financials may see a boost from additional M&A and mortgage activity that may come about from lower rates,” Schein added.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.12%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.59%.

On the economic side, initial jobless claims dropped more than expected in the past week. Also, the Philly Fed Manufacturing Index jumped in September, blowing past consensus.

The August U.S. Leading Economic Index fell more than expected in August, -0.5% MoM to 98.4.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Nvidia's massive investment creates 'game changer' moment for Intel: Wedbush
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares have been shocked back to life following massive investments by the U.S. government, SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY)(OTCPK:SFTBF) and now today's $5B deal by the world's artificial intelligence chip leader Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA). 

"This is a game changer deal for Intel as it now brings them front and center into the AI game," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a Thursday investor note. "Along with the recent US Government investment for 10% this has been a golden few weeks for Intel after years of pain and frustration for investors."

Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares surged 25% when the market opened on Thursday. If the gains hold, Intel will close at its highest value since July 2024. Shares are now up more than 50% year to date. 

"The companies are incrementally focused on connecting NVDA and INTC architectures, leveraging NVDA's AI strength and accelerated computing with Intel's CPU tech ecosystem for improved technological solutions for its customers," Ives said. 

"While not announcing a specific timeline for when these parts go on sale, this announcement does not impact the individual growth strategies of NVDA and INTC moving forward as both companies look to capitalize on growing demand for CPUs and GPUs within the AI Revolution," he added.

The announcement has also helped catapult Intel from a "laggard into a catalyst" and strengthened the U.S. lead in the global AI race, according to Wedbush.

"With AI infrastructure investments continuing to grow with the company expecting between $3 trillion to $4 trillion in total AI infrastructure spend by the end of the decade, the chip landscape remains NVDA's world with everybody else paying rent as more sovereigns and enterprises wait in line for the most advanced chips in the world," Ives said.

The two companies will hold a press conference at 1 p.m. EST to discuss the deal.

data.title: U.S. Leading Indicator Index falls more than consensus in August
data.description:
The August U.S. Leading Economic Index: -0.5% M/M to 98.4 vs. -0.1% consensus and 98.9 in July (revised from 98.7).

Coincident Index: 115.0 vs. 114.8 in July (revised from 114.9).

Lagging Index: 120.0 vs. 119.9 prior (unchanged).

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: PepsiCo, Peloton, and Dutch Bros are on UBS' list of top consumer stocks
data.description:
UBS named its top stock picks out of the consumer sector. The firm highlighted 7 stocks where it said it has a differentiated view based on interesting or proprietary data sources.

PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP): "We believe PEP represents a compelling rate-of-change story over the next 12-18 months and is one of the few large-cap Staples names with a defensible case for multiple expansion. With potential for a return to on-algo growth in 2H26 along with +HSD EPS growth for '26, we think the risk/reward for PEP is among the most attractive in the entire group."

J.M. Smucker Company (SJM): "Our analysis suggests that favorable underlying category growth dynamics coupled with the company's ability to take price in the coffee segment will drive +MSD organic growth and more than offset continued pressures from Hostess, with our organic growth estimate of +5.6% ahead of consensus (+5.5%). From a bottom line perspective, we believe cost savings, potential pricing, and favorable below-the-line dynamics pave a path to solid long-term EPS growth that could result in upside to current estimates."

Albertsons (ACI): "We believe ACI’s pullback has been overdone, & doesn't reflect its opportunity for upside. It’s now past the distraction from its merger attempt with Kroger. It’s seen 15 straight quarters of double-digit pharmacy growth. These shoppers tend to have some of Albertsons strongest lifetime customer values. It’s also making price investments, that should help to spur grocery SSS growth."

Dutch Bros (NYSE:BROS): "We believe BROS shares remain positioned for further upside given industry leading store growth and ongoing sales and traffic momentum likely sustainable through 2H25 and ’26 supported by a compelling catalyst path. We expect BROS should maintain market share gains in a high-growth category (~10% category sales CAGR '21-'24) enabling 20%+ revenue growth, supported by strong customer brand affinity and drivers to sustain momentum, including compelling new products, marketing effectiveness, mobile order, operational enhancements, and a ’26 food launch." 

Ulta Beauty (ULTA): "ULTA is demonstrating strong execution under its new CEO, with improved internal operations and a favorable competitive backdrop, as evidenced by 2Q comp acceleration. Manageable comp comparisons in 2H and a robust pipeline of product newness support sustained momentum and potential outperformance. For FY24, margin expansion presents a key opportunity, with SG&A investments expected to moderate, driving positive earnings revisions." 

On Holding (ONON): "We think On's continued focus on innovation, performance, athletes, sports, direct-to-consumer selling, and maintaining a premium, full-price brand image will lead to industry leading sales growth and margin beats. Plus, we think ONON is poised to navigate tariffs better than most Softlines companies."

Peloton Interactive (NASDAQ:PTON): "We have a Buy rating on PTON with a PT of $11, supported by: 1) upside to '26E EBITDA driven by revenue growth and cost efficiencies; 2) positive trends in key underlying metrics; and 3) favorable risk/reward, with the stock trading at ~6-7x EBITDA as cash flows inflect. Subscriber growth inflection is critical for multiple expansion, requiring improvement in subs trajectory despite potential churn from price increases during seasonally important months."

Digging into UBS' list of top consumer stocks, Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) is the only one with a clean sweep of buy ratings from Seeking Alpha analysts, Wall Street analysts, and the Seeking Alpha Quant Rating system.

data.title: Replimune provides update on FDA meeting
data.description:
• Replimune Group (NASDAQ:REPL) on Thursday said it held a Type A meeting with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration [FDA] on September 16 to discuss the complete response letter [CRL] for the company’s Biologics License Application [BLA] for RP1 in combination with nivolumab for the treatment of advanced melanoma.

  • The company is evaluating the feedback from the FDA provided during the meeting to determine next steps.
  • "We remain committed to working with the FDA to determine an expeditious path forward for RP1", CEO Sushil Patel, said in a statement.
  • REPL is -38.52% to $3.51
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amazon expands multi-channel fulfillment to Shein, Shopify, and Walmart third party merchants
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) will now offer third-party merchants the ability to fulfill orders from other sales channels including Walmart (WMT), SHEIN, and Shopify (SHOP) through Amazon’s (NASDAQ:AMZN) Multi-Channel Fulfillment (MCF).

Amazon’s MCF gives merchants the option to have Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) pick, pack, and ship their orders from channels beyond Amazon.com, enabling merchants to give their customers the same experience as if ordered directly from Amazon.

This expansion to Walmart (WMT), SHEIN, and Shopify (SHOP) is part of the company’s focus on helping merchants build and scale their businesses on Amazon.com, as well as on other sales channels. According to Amazon (AMZN), merchants have reported an average 19% increase in sales, as well as reduced out-of-stock rates and improved inventory turnover from one shared pool of inventory.

By acting as a third-party logistics provider, Amazon (AMZN) generates revenue through its MCF with monthly storage fees, along with pick, packing, and shipping fees. The latter of which vary based on the size and weight of the merchandise. For merchants that sell products directly from the Amazon marketplace, the company charges a 15% or higher referral fee.   

By the end of the year, Amazon (AMZN) will make it easier for SHEIN merchants to choose Amazon MCF to fulfill marketplace orders through a free app in Amazon Seller Central, Amazon Supply Chain Portal, and the SHEIN seller hub.

Amazon MCF is now available for merchants to fulfill orders from Walmart (WMT) and Shopify (SHOP).

Source: Press Release

data.title: DexCom falls after short report from Hunterbrook
data.description:
• DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM) dropped 3% after a short report from Hunterbrook Capital.
  • Hunterbrook Capital disclosed that it's short shares of DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM).
  • DexCom didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.

data.title: GMTech in talks with WebKey Group for Web3 mobile collaboration
data.description:
• GMTech (OTCPK:GMTH) on Thursday said it is in discussions with WebKey Group Corporation about a potential collaboration in the Web3 mobile ecosystem.
  • The companies are exploring joint efforts in digital asset management, blockchain integration, and on-chain financial infrastructure.
  • WebKey, which focuses on Web3 smartphones and blockchain-based digital solutions, could complement GMTech’s strategy to develop smart hardware and digital finance solutions.
  • The talks are in early stages, and no agreements have been finalized.
  • GMTH is -21.0% to $0.079.
  • Source: Press Release
